title: Claude Opus 4.8 Support
description: Bring agent-bober up to the Claude Opus 4.8 release: make the `opus` shorthand resolve to claude-opus-4-8 (with a pinned opus-4-7 alias), upgrade the Anthropic SDK to a version exposing the new request fields, and add support for the two new Messages API capabilities — the output_config.effort control and mid-conversation system blocks. This keeps every opus-role agent on the latest model and exposes the new cost/quality and cache-preserving controls.
assumptions: Target repo is the sibling /Users/bober4ik/agent-bober-workspace/agent-bober; the cockpit consumes the rebuilt dist via npm link.
The `opus` shorthand should track the latest Opus (claude-opus-4-8); a new `opus-4-7` shorthand preserves explicit pins to the prior model.
claude-opus-4-8 is a dateless pinned snapshot (Claude 4.6+ naming), so no dated alias suffix is needed.

The model bump (sprint 1) works on the currently-installed @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.39.0 because model is passed to messages.create as a free string the API accepts server-side; the SDK upgrade is only required for the effort and mid_conv_system features.

Per the API reference there is NO role:'system' inside the messages array; mid-conversation instructions use a mid_conv_system content block ({ type:'mid_conv_system', content:[{type:'text',text}], cache_control?:{type:'ephemeral',ttl} }).
effort lives at top-level output_config.effort with values low|medium|high|xhigh|max and defaults to high on Opus 4.8, so omitting it preserves current behavior.

The Anthropic SDK upgrade target is the current latest (0.100.1) or the minimum version that types output_config.effort and the mid_conv_system block; @anthropic-ai/sdk stays imported only inside src/providers/anthropic.ts.
outOfScope: Changing the adapter's default max_tokens (Opus 4.8 supports 128k output; the 16384 default is left untouched)
Wiring a per-role effort setting into bober.config.json schema (core support lands in ChatParams + adapter; config exposure is deferred)
Adding extended-thinking / thinking-parameter support (Opus 4.8 uses adaptive thinking, not the extended-thinking toggle)
Updating other providers (OpenAI/Google) to new features beyond gracefully ignoring the new agnostic fields
The 300k extended-output beta header and Batch API changes
Updating prose docs (README/AGENTS.md) model references beyond what is needed for code + tests

features.title: Opus 4.8 as the default opus model
features.description: Repoint the `opus` shorthand to claude-opus-4-8 and add a pinned `opus-4-7` alias so existing pins still resolve. Ships independently of the SDK upgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When the `opus` shorthand is resolved, resolveProviderModel returns provider anthropic and modelId claude-opus-4-8.
AC2: When the `opus-4-7` shorthand is resolved, resolveProviderModel returns provider anthropic and modelId claude-opus-4-7.
AC3: model-resolver.test.ts asserts the opus->4.8 mapping and the opus-4-7 alias, and the full unit-test suite stays green.

features.title: Upgrade @anthropic-ai/sdk for the new API fields
features.description: Bump @anthropic-ai/sdk from 0.39.0 to a version whose types expose output_config.effort and the mid_conv_system content block, and fix any breaking changes in the Anthropic adapter while keeping all existing behavior (including prompt caching) intact.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: package.json declares an @anthropic-ai/sdk version whose published types include output_config.effort and the mid_conv_system content block, and package-lock is updated to match.
AC2: After the upgrade, the Anthropic adapter compiles and npm run typecheck, lint, and build all exit zero.
AC3: Every existing provider unit test passes, including the prompt-caching tests, with no behavior change apart from the documented flaky baseline failures.

features.title: Effort control (output_config.effort)
features.description: Add an optional, provider-agnostic effort field to ChatParams and have the Anthropic adapter forward it as output_config.effort. Omitting it sends no output_config so the API default (high on 4.8) applies.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: ChatParams gains an optional effort field typed to the union low | medium | high | xhigh | max.
AC2: When effort is set, the Anthropic request body includes output_config.effort equal to that value, verified by a unit test capturing messages.create.
AC3: When effort is unset, the Anthropic request body contains no output_config field.
AC4: Passing effort to a non-anthropic adapter is ignored and raises no error.

features.title: Mid-conversation system blocks
features.description: Add a provider-agnostic Message variant for a mid-conversation system instruction, rendered by the Anthropic adapter as a mid_conv_system content block (with optional ephemeral cache_control) so instructions can be updated mid-task without breaking the prompt cache.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: The provider Message union gains a variant carrying mid-conversation system text plus an optional ephemeral cache TTL.
AC2: The Anthropic adapter renders that variant as a content block of type mid_conv_system whose content is a text block with the supplied text.
AC3: When the variant supplies a cache TTL, the emitted mid_conv_system block carries cache_control of type ephemeral with that ttl; otherwise no cache_control is attached.
AC4: A non-anthropic adapter handles the variant without throwing (best-effort text rendering or a documented skip).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The SDK upgrade and new fields must not regress existing provider behavior; the disabled/absent paths for effort and mid_conv_system must match prior request payloads.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Run the full provider unit-test suite plus typecheck/lint/build; assert unset-effort and no-system-update payloads contain no output_config / mid_conv_system.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: @anthropic-ai/sdk and all Anthropic SDK types stay confined to src/providers/anthropic.ts; new agnostic fields live in providers/types.ts.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: grep that @anthropic-ai/sdk is imported only in anthropic.ts; confirm ChatParams/Message changes are in types.ts.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: No prompt content or API keys are newly logged by the upgraded adapter or new fields.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Inspect the adapter diff for any added logging of request bodies or credentials.

integrationPoints: Anthropic Messages API: top-level output_config.effort (low|medium|high|xhigh|max, default high on Opus 4.8); mid_conv_system content block ({type:'mid_conv_system', content:[{type:'text',text}], cache_control?:{type:'ephemeral',ttl:'5m'}})
@anthropic-ai/sdk upgrade 0.39.0 -> latest (0.100.1) for typed support of the above
src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts SHORTHAND_MAP for the model bump
techNotes.dataModel: ChatParams gains optional effort: 'low'|'medium'|'high'|'xhigh'|'max'. The Message union gains a mid-conversation-system variant carrying text + optional ephemeral cache ttl. No persisted data.
securityConsiderations: Keep @anthropic-ai/sdk imports inside the adapter only; do not log request bodies or keys.
techNotes.existingPatterns: model-resolver SHORTHAND_MAP (src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts:22); provider-agnostic types (src/providers/types.ts); Anthropic conversion + the recently-added prompt-caching helpers in src/providers/anthropic.ts; per-provider *.test.ts vitest pattern with a mocked SDK.

metadata.riskNotes: Sprint 1 (model bump) is trivial and ships independently. The real risk is sprint 2: jumping @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.39.0 -> 0.100.1 may introduce breaking changes in messages.create signatures, content-block/usage types, and the just-added cache_control wiring; it carries strong regression gates. Sprint 4 (mid_conv_system) is the most architecturally involved and lowest immediate ROI given agent-bober resets context per subagent.
metadata.sourceAnnouncement: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8 (Opus 4.8 GA 2026-05-28); shapes confirmed against platform.claude.com API reference.
